question: Which is greater: 2/4 or 3/4?
hints: The denominators are the same. Compare the numerators.
More pieces of the same size means a larger fraction.

question: Which is greater: 5/8 or 3/8?
hints: Same denominator (8). Which numerator is bigger?
solution: 5/8 > 3/8. Same-sized pieces (eighths), so more pieces (5) is greater.

question: Which is greater: 1/3 or 1/6?
hints: Fewer pieces means each piece is bigger.
solution: 1/3 > 1/6. Same numerator, so the smaller denominator means bigger pieces.

question: Compare 4/6 and 2/6. Use >, <, or =.
solution: 4/6 > 2/6. Both are sixths, so 4 sixths is more than 2 sixths.

question: Put these fractions in order from least to greatest: 1/8, 1/2, 1/4.
hints: Bigger denominator = smaller pieces = smaller fraction.
solution: 1/8 < 1/4 < 1/2. Same numerator, so larger denominator means smaller fraction. Order: 1/8, 1/4, 1/2.
